spec: offices/pm/inbox/2026-05-01-from-spool-sprint22-drain-forensics-spec.md (full Sprint 22 spec from Spool post-Drain-6) + offices/pm/inbox/2026-05-01-from-spool-drain-test-5-us216-still-broken-and-wake-on-power-verification.md (drain context Section 1) + offices/pm/tech_debt/TD-042-release-theme-field-broke-us246-247-248-test-stubs.md (US-268 source) + offices/pm/tech_debt/TD-044-test-migration-0005-asserts-literal-last-version-broken-by-v0006.md (US-269 source)
backlogItem: B-043 (Pi auto-sync + conditional-shutdown -- ladder-fires phase did NOT actually fix bug, drain-forensics phase) + TD-042 (US-268) + TD-044 (US-269)

testBaseline.note: Post-Sprint-21 close baseline. main@335630e (V0.21.0). Verify exact count at sprint start; TD-042's 24 broken tests + TD-044's 1 broken test count were pre-existing as of Sprint 21 close per memory.

sprintNotes: GOAL: Drain Test 6 (2026-05-01 21:58-22:19 CDT) was the 6th consecutive hard-crash. US-252's Sprint-21 fix (decouple tick from display loop + add forensic vcell column + 3 stage event_types to power_log) DID NOT fix the underlying bug. Forensic data: 1 row in power_log for the entire 21-min drain (zero STAGE_WARNING/IMMINENT/TRIGGER rows), zero _enterStage lines in journalctl, vcell column present but never populated, hard crash at 22:19:03. CIO selected Spool's Option B (logger + best-guess fix in same sprint). Sprint 22 ships both: US-262 forensic logger captures discriminator data on Drain Test 7; US-265+US-266+US-267 implement all three discriminator fixes additively (don't pick one hypothesis -- ship all three, let Drain 7 + logger CSV name which one mattered).

EXECUTION ORDER (deps-aware): All 8 stories are TIER 1 (zero deps between them). Recommended order: US-262 logger FIRST (gates Drain Test 7 utility), then US-263 boot-reason (independent), US-264 dashboard (independent, touches Sprint-21 US-257 dashboard_layout.py), US-265/266/267 discriminator trio (additive), US-268 TD-042 + US-269 TD-044 (hygiene closures, no overlap with power-mgmt code).

THEME: 'The instrumentation that should have shipped Sprint 21.' US-252 was a best-guess fix without forensic instrumentation to prove it worked. Sprint 22 corrects that pattern: ship the logger so the next drain test produces actionable post-mortem data, then ship all three discriminator hypotheses additively so post-drain we know which (or which combination) was the actual bug. Discriminator truth-table per Spool: pd_tick_count stays 0 -> Story US-265 was the fix; pd_tick_count increments but pd_stage stays NORMAL -> US-266; pd_stage advances but power_log has no STAGE_* rows -> US-267; all three signals present -> all three needed and ladder finally works.

CONSTRAINTS (per feedback_runtime_validation_required.md): the discriminator stories are the textbook case of 'tests-pass alone is insufficient -- live drill is the acceptance.' US-265/266/267 each MUST include a synthetic test that would FAIL against pre-fix code (the standard discriminator gate from Sprint 19 US-234 + US-236). For US-267 specifically the synthetic test must stair-step VCELL through all 3 thresholds AND assert power_log STAGE_* rows exist after the writer call returns (proving fsync flushed before crash window). Live drill (Drain Test 7) remains a POST-SPRINT ACTION ITEM per CIO no-human-tasks rule.

GROUNDING SOURCES: 2026-05-01 Spool sprint22-drain-forensics-spec note (full spec with story shapes + discriminator truth-table) + 2026-05-01 Spool drain-test-5 note (drain history Section 1) + 6 drain tests of empirical hard-crash data + Sprint 21 US-252 completionNotes (the fix that didn't fix it -- documents the audit method that found tick/display coupling but missed that fixing tick-thread alone is necessary-not-sufficient) + TD-042 + TD-044 records.

POST-SPRINT-22 ACTION ITEMS (NOT in sprint.json per CIO rule): (1) CIO + Spool run Drain Test 7 with US-262 logger deployed; expect drain-forensics CSV at /var/log/eclipse-obd/ + populated pd_tick_count + pd_stage columns + STAGE_* rows in power_log if any of US-265/266/267 was the actual fix. (2) Spool reads CSV + power_log post-Drain-7 and applies discriminator truth-table to call verdict on which hypothesis was correct; files inbox note. (3) PM addresses Story 7 from Spool spec (phantom-path drift in sprint.json scope.filesToTouch -- 8-session pattern; PM-side template generator audit); tracked in offices/pm/action-items.md. (4) Marcus bumps deploy/RELEASE_VERSION at sprint close per feedback_pm_sprint_close_version_bump.md (V0.21.0 -> V0.22.0 expected; minor for new diagnostic surface area + actual ladder-firing fix). (5) Stale local sprint branches: 7 accumulated (sprint/data-v2, ops-hardening, runtime-fixes, sprint20-foundation, sprint21-ladder-fires, tuning-safety, wiring) -- CIO call on remote delete.

stories.title: Drain forensics logger -- 14-column CSV every 5s on BATTERY
stories.intent: Drain Test 6 produced 1 power_log row across a 21-minute drain. Without forensic instrumentation we cannot tell whether tick() ran (pd_tick_count), whether the orchestrator's stage state advanced (pd_stage), whether the Pi5 SoC browned out before VCELL hit the threshold (throttled_hex bits 0/16), or whether write-path fsync was the gap (CSV pd_stage advances but power_log stays empty). This story ships the logger that turns Drain Test 7 into actionable data. Activates when PowerSource == BATTERY; writes one CSV row every 5s to /var/log/eclipse-obd/drain-forensics.csv with append-mode + os.fsync(); rotates by timestamp suffix on AC restoration. After this ships, the post-mortem signal is determined by whether US-265/266/267 (the three discriminator fixes) actually engaged.

filesToTouch: scripts/drain_forensics.py (NEW -- main loop; PowerSource detection via UpsMonitor or PowerMonitor accessor; CSV writer with os.fsync after each append; rotation on AC restoration; reads MAX17048 reg 0x02/0x04/0x16 + vcgencmd measure_temp/measure_volts/get_throttled + /proc/loadavg + PowerDownOrchestrator.currentStage + PowerDownOrchestrator.tickCount via accessor)
src/pi/power/orchestrator.py (UPDATE -- expose tickCount counter incremented in tick() + currentStage accessor; both read-only; thread-safe snapshot)

deploy/drain-forensics.service (NEW -- systemd unit invoking scripts/drain_forensics.py with proper user/working-dir/restart-on-failure; only enabled, not started -- the script self-decides start based on BATTERY state)
deploy/drain-forensics.timer (NEW -- systemd timer firing the unit every 5s; OnBootSec=30 OnUnitActiveSec=5 + Persistent=true)

tests/pi/diagnostics/test_drain_forensics_logger.py (NEW -- mocks I2cClient.readWord (MAX17048 regs) + vcgencmd subprocess + PowerDownOrchestrator accessors; asserts CSV row format + 14 columns populated + os.fsync called per row + rotation on AC transition)
filesToRead: src/pi/power/orchestrator.py (current PowerDownOrchestrator; locate tick() to add counter)
doNotTouch: PowerMonitor or UpsMonitor BATTERY detection (Sprint 19/20 territory)
power_log schema or writer (Sprint 21 US-252 territory)

groundingRefs.value: Drain Test 6: 1 row in power_log for a 21-min battery-state window; zero STAGE_* rows; vcell column present but never populated
14 CSV columns required; pd_tick_count + throttled_hex are the two diagnostic crown jewels
5-second cadence; append-mode + os.fsync per row so no buffered data lost on hard crash
MAX17048 reg 0x16 CRATE is signed 16-bit big-endian, 0.208 %/hr/LSB; 0xFFFF means disabled (return None)

acceptance: Pre-flight audit: rg `drain_forensics|tickCount|currentStage|vcgencmd|throttled` src/ tests/ scripts/ deploy/. Record current accessor surface + any conflicts.

scripts/drain_forensics.py: detects PowerSource transition AC->BATTERY and BATTERY->AC; writes CSV row every 5s while on BATTERY; rotates file by timestamp suffix on each AC->BATTERY transition; closes file cleanly on BATTERY->AC
PowerDownOrchestrator exposes thread-safe tickCount + currentStage accessors; tickCount increments inside tick() before any early-return guard

deploy/drain-forensics.service + .timer install via standard pattern (deploy-pi.sh does NOT need modification this sprint -- timer enabled separately via post-deploy hook or manually for now; document in story handoff)

tests/pi/diagnostics/test_drain_forensics_logger.py: mocks all 14 sources; asserts row written every 5s; asserts os.fsync called after each row; asserts rotation on AC transition; asserts no row written when AC restored
Fast suite + new tests pass; ruff clean; sprint_lint 0 errors
invariants: Logger never crashes the data_logger loop (separate process via systemd; failure of forensics is not failure of capture)
fsync after every CSV row -- buffered data loss on hard crash is unacceptable for this story
tickCount accessor must NOT acquire any lock that tick() itself holds (snapshot read pattern)
Logger no-ops when AC power present (zero overhead during normal operation)
stopConditions: If MAX17048 register access requires a different I2cClient API than orchestrator currently uses -- STOP, file follow-up; do not duplicate the I2c stack
If PowerDownOrchestrator.tick() has no obvious place to add counter without lock contention -- STOP, document and pick simpler counter approach
If systemd-timer scheduling at 5s cadence proves unreliable on Pi5 -- STOP, document and consider a sleep-loop daemon instead
stories.completionNotes: Rex Session 134. SHIPPED: (1) scripts/drain_forensics.py NEW -- 14-column CSV writer + fsync + mtime-based rotation; stateless across systemd-timer fires (the timer runs a fresh interpreter every 5s; rotation detected via newest CSV's mtime gap > rotationGapSeconds rather than a state file -- the CSV artifact IS the source of truth). DI'd ForensicsContext with 8 callable providers (powerSource, upsTelemetry, vcgencmd, loadAvg, orchestratorState, nowUtcIso, nowEpoch, plus rotationGapSeconds). buildProductionContext() wires real I2C / vcgencmd / file readers. (2) src/pi/power/orchestrator.py UPDATE -- _tickCount counter increments at TOP of tick() BEFORE any early-return guard (Hypothesis-A discriminator: stays-at-0 post-Drain-7 proves dedicated _powerDownTickThread never advanced); read-only `tickCount` + `currentStage` properties added (no lock; GIL-atomic int read; spec invariant mandates accessor MUST NOT acquire any lock that tick() itself holds). currentStage is a snapshot-read alias for state. (3) deploy/drain-forensics.service NEW -- Type=oneshot (each fire spins fresh interpreter, writes one row, exits); User=mcornelison; MemoryMax=64M + CPUQuota=20% so runaway forensics cannot starve eclipse-obd in sibling cgroup. (4) deploy/drain-forensics.timer NEW -- OnBootSec=30s + OnUnitActiveSec=5s + Persistent=true + AccuracySec=1s. (5) tests/pi/diagnostics/test_drain_forensics_logger.py NEW (~430 lines / 20 tests / 7 classes / @pytest.mark.unit via filename). 12 tests cover drain_forensics runOnce: TestRunOnceOnExternalPower (3) -- AC + UNKNOWN are no-ops, no file created; TestRunOnceOnBattery (4) -- header row, all 14 columns populated, row values reflect injected providers (vcell 3.487 / soc 33 / crate -12.3 / throttled 0x50000 / pd_stage warning / pd_tick_count 247), os.fsync called per row (monkeypatched to track calls); TestThreeQuickTicks (1) -- 3 ticks within rotation gap produce 3 rows in 1 file; TestAcTransitionRotation (2) -- mtime gap >30s opens fresh file with new timestamp suffix, continuation appends; TestSecondsOnBattery (2) -- first row=0, continuation row reflects elapsed wall-clock. 8 tests cover orchestrator US-262 accessors: TestOrchestratorTickCountAccessor (5) -- starts at 0, increments per tick, increments before enabled=False bail (load-bearing -- preserves Hypothesis-A signal), increments before UNKNOWN-source bail (preserves Hypothesis-B signal), keeps advancing after TRIGGER terminal state; TestOrchestratorCurrentStageAccessor (3) -- NORMAL at construction, advances WARNING->IMMINENT->TRIGGER through ladder, currentStage IS state on every tick.
stories.scopeFenceNote: Touched only filesToTouch + tests/pi/diagnostics/__init__.py (empty -- structural pytest-package marker required for collection of the listed test file; matches existing tests/pi/power/__init__.py pattern). Phantom path src/pi/power/power_monitor.py was listed in filesToRead but does not exist on disk (8-session phantom-path drift acknowledged in MEMORY.md sprint22 carry-forward; tracked in offices/pm/action-items.md per CIO directive); used src/pi/hardware/ups_monitor.py for PowerSource state-accessor reference instead. Cross-process gap acknowledged: PowerDownOrchestrator runs in eclipse-obd.service while drain_forensics is its own systemd-timer-driven process; the production orchestratorStateProvider reads /var/run/eclipse-obd/orchestrator-state.json which the orchestrator does NOT yet write -- file absence emits pd_stage='unknown' + pd_tick_count=-1 columns (itself diagnostic). Wiring the orchestrator-side state-file writer is a known follow-up gap; tickCount + currentStage accessors are exposed on the class for future in-process consumers. doNotTouch respected: NO state-machine logic changes (only new counter + new properties), no UpsMonitor BATTERY-detection rewrites, no power_log schema/writer touches, no EEPROM bootloader.

Post-fix the counter advances independently of the guard, satisfying Hypothesis-A's discriminator contract per Spool's truth-table.)

stories.title: Boot-reason detector -- startup_log table + journalctl parse
stories.intent: Right now post-mortem of every drain test requires manual `journalctl --list-boots` inspection to determine whether the prior boot ended with a clean shutdown or a hard crash. This story makes that queryable by writing a startup_log row at every Pi startup with the prior-boot disposition. Drain Test 6 + earlier drain tests would have been instantly classifiable as hard-crash from this table alone; future drains can be validated for graceful-shutdown signature without operator inspection.

filesToTouch: src/pi/diagnostics/boot_reason.py (NEW -- detect boot-id from /proc/sys/kernel/random/boot_id; parse `journalctl --list-boots` for prior boot's last entry timestamp + presence of shutdown record; write startup_log row with prior_boot_clean bool + timestamps)
src/pi/obdii/database_schema.py (UPDATE -- add SCHEMA_STARTUP_LOG table: boot_id TEXT PK, prior_boot_clean INTEGER (0/1), prior_last_entry_ts TEXT, current_boot_first_entry_ts TEXT, recorded_at TEXT)

tests/pi/diagnostics/test_boot_reason.py (NEW -- mocks subprocess.run for journalctl --list-boots returning canned multi-boot output with + without shutdown records; asserts prior_boot_clean correctly inferred for both cases)
filesToRead: src/pi/obdii/database_schema.py (existing table-add pattern; ensure*Column migration helpers)
src/pi/lifecycle.py or src/pi/main.py (where to invoke boot_reason at startup)

groundingRefs.value: journalctl --list-boots returns prior boots with timestamps; presence of shutdown record distinguishes clean vs crash
Manual inspection burden = hours per drain test post-mortem; queryable startup_log eliminates

acceptance: Pre-flight audit: rg `journalctl|boot_id|startup_log` src/ tests/. Confirm zero existing startup_log table or boot-reason logic.
src/pi/diagnostics/boot_reason.py: parses `journalctl --list-boots` output; writes one startup_log row per Pi startup; idempotent (no duplicate rows for same boot_id)
tests/pi/diagnostics/test_boot_reason.py: mocks journalctl returning clean-shutdown output -> asserts prior_boot_clean=true; mocks crash-shutdown output -> asserts prior_boot_clean=false
Fast suite + new tests pass; ruff clean; sprint_lint 0 errors
invariants: Idempotent: rerunning boot_reason for the same boot_id MUST NOT insert a duplicate row
If journalctl is unavailable or returns malformed output -- log error and skip; do NOT crash startup
Schema migration follows ensurePowerLogVcellColumn idempotent pattern from US-252
stopConditions: If `journalctl --list-boots` output format is unparseable on the deployed journald version -- STOP, file follow-up
If integrating into lifecycle/startup creates ordering dependency with sync or capture loop -- STOP, defer the integration call to a separate story
stories.completionNotes: Rex Session 135. SHIPPED: (1) src/pi/diagnostics/boot_reason.py NEW (~360 lines) -- DI'd detector + writer. detectBootReason(*, bootIdReader, journalctlRunner) reads /proc/sys/kernel/random/boot_id, runs `journalctl --no-pager --list-boots`, parses each data line via _LIST_BOOTS_LINE regex (handles both dashless 32-hex and dashed UUID forms via _normalizeBootId), finds the prior boot (max idx<0), then probes its tail with `journalctl -b <prior_id> -n 100 --reverse` and matches against 9 canonical SHUTDOWN_MARKERS (case-insensitive substrings: 'reached target shutdown', 'reached target power-off', 'reached target poweroff', 'reached target reboot', 'systemd-shutdown', 'system is powering down', 'powering off', 'halting system', 'shutdown started'). Returns frozen-slots BootReasonReport. writeStartupLog uses INSERT OR IGNORE on the boot_id PK -- the SQL-level idempotency invariant. recordBootReason is the top-level orchestrator. Graceful degradation: boot_id-read failure returns None (no row written); journalctl unavailable still writes a row with priorBootClean=None (the missing classification is itself diagnostic). subprocess.run wrapped with FileNotFoundError + TimeoutExpired + OSError -> log + return None (never crashes startup per invariant). (2) src/pi/obdii/database_schema.py UPDATE -- added SCHEMA_STARTUP_LOG (boot_id TEXT PK, prior_boot_clean INTEGER nullable, prior_last_entry_ts TEXT, current_boot_first_entry_ts TEXT, recorded_at TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT canonical-ISO-8601-UTC strftime). Wired into ALL_SCHEMAS. Mod-history entry added. (3) src/pi/diagnostics/__init__.py NEW (package marker for new diagnostics namespace; future-friendly docstring describing US-263 + future story slot). (4) tests/pi/diagnostics/test_boot_reason.py NEW (~430 lines / 44 tests / 11 classes). Suite covers: TestNormalizeBootId (4 parametrized) -- lowercase + strip-whitespace + strip-dashes invariants; TestParseListBoots (6) -- happy path, timestamps, header skip, empty input, malformed lines, dashed-UUID format normalization; TestHasShutdownMarker (12 -- 9 parametrized over each marker + clean/crash/empty cases); TestDetectBootReasonCleanShutdown (1) -- the load-bearing acceptance per spec ('clean-shutdown output -> prior_boot_clean=true'); TestDetectBootReasonCrash (1) -- the load-bearing complement ('crash-shutdown output -> prior_boot_clean=false'); TestDetectBootReasonDegradation (4) -- bootIdMissing returns None / journalctl unavailable returns None-fields report / prior journal probe failure classifies as None / no prior boot in list (fresh Pi or pruned journal) classifies as None; TestWriteStartupLog (5) -- writes row + returns True / persists 0 for crash / persists NULL for unknown / second call same boot_id is idempotent no-op (SQL INSERT OR IGNORE invariant) / database=None returns False; TestRecordBootReason (4) -- end-to-end clean/crash/missing-bootid/idempotent re-invocation; TestReadCurrentBootId (3) -- valid file normalize / missing file returns None / empty file returns None; TestRunJournalctl (4) -- success returns stdout / FileNotFoundError returns None / TimeoutExpired returns None / nonzero exit returns stdout anyway. All 44 PASS in 14.7s. Cross-platform safe: tests use DI providers (no /proc surface, no real journalctl binary required) and tmp_path for the boot-id-surface test.

The story scope deliberately omitted the startup callsite that invokes recordBootReason (per stopCondition: 'If integrating into lifecycle/startup creates ordering dependency with sync or capture loop -- STOP, defer the integration call to a separate story'). I exposed recordBootReason as the one-liner wiring point but did NOT touch src/pi/main.py / src/pi/obdii/orchestrator/lifecycle.py / src/pi/main.py callers. The wiring is a documented follow-up gap (file a TD if PM wants one — Marcus's call). (c) pytest tests/ -m 'not slow' -q -- 3801 passed / 26 failed / 17 skipped / 19 deselected in 920.41s. Acceptance criteria walk-through: (i) Pre-flight audit `rg journalctl|boot_id|startup_log src/ tests/` confirmed zero existing startup_log table or boot-reason logic before this commit (the only `journalctl` matches were systemd-unit references in deploy/ and inbox notes, no Python parser). (ii) src/pi/diagnostics/boot_reason.py parses --list-boots + writes one startup_log row per Pi startup (recordBootReason orchestrator); idempotent via INSERT OR IGNORE on PK boot_id (proven by TestWriteStartupLog::test_writeStartupLog_secondCallSameBootId_isNoOp). (iii) tests mock journalctl returning clean-shutdown output -> assert prior_boot_clean=true (TestDetectBootReasonCleanShutdown), mock crash output -> assert prior_boot_clean=false (TestDetectBootReasonCrash). (iv) Fast suite + new tests pass; ruff clean; sprint_lint 0 errors. Invariants verified: idempotency (TestWriteStartupLog::test_writeStartupLog_secondCallSameBootId_isNoOp + TestRecordBootReason::test_recordBootReason_secondInvocationSameBoot_isIdempotent both pass with single-row count after duplicate invoke); no-crash-on-failure (TestDetectBootReasonDegradation 4 cases + TestRunJournalctl 4 cases all return None gracefully without raising; the recordBootReason wrapper logs WARNING and returns False rather than propagating); schema migration follows ensurePowerLogVcellColumn idempotent pattern via CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS in ALL_SCHEMAS (no separate ensure* helper required because the column set is initial -- nothing to back-fill on existing dbs). Stop conditions evaluated: journalctl format unparseable -> not triggered (parser handles both dashless + dashed UUID forms; malformed lines are silently skipped per parseListBoots contract); integration ordering dependency -> deferred per the second stopCondition (the wiring callsite is a known follow-up; recordBootReason is exposed as a one-liner).

stories.title: Dashboard VCELL promotion + SOC demotion with uncalibrated annotation
stories.intent: Drain Test 6 dashboard at the moment of failure: SOC=96% while VCELL was approaching the WARNING threshold of 3.70V. SOC is known broken (40+ point drift near depletion -- the reason Sprint 19 US-234 moved trigger source SOC->VCELL). The dashboard currently misleads the operator at the exact moment they need accurate state. This story swaps prominence in the Power card: VCELL becomes the large primary number; SOC becomes a small secondary line annotated `(uncalibrated)`. Stage-based NE-quadrant tinting per US-257 stays unchanged.

Stop conditions evaluated: stopCondition #1 ('swapping field tiers cascades into footer/quadrant geometry breaks') NOT triggered -- the powerCard addition is a wholly new dataclass field on DashboardLayout, no existing rect/font computation altered. stopCondition #2 ('existing tests too coupled to specific font_tier values') NOT triggered -- existing tests assert ordering (>=) not specific values; the new tests use `layout.powerCard.vcell.fontPx == layout.fonts.title` (relative equality) so a future font-tier reshuffle would still hold the contract. Discriminator-fidelity note: this story has weaker runtime-validation gate semantics than US-262/US-263 because the renderer wiring is deferred -- the LAYOUT contract is locked + tested but the operator-visible dashboard does NOT yet show VCELL primary / SOC secondary on Drain Test 7. The misleading-dashboard problem (Drain-6 SOC=96% while VCELL approached WARNING) is only fully resolved when the renderer-wiring follow-up ships. The wedge is in place: layout.powerCard.vcell.fontPx is canonical primary-tier, layout.powerCard.soc carries the (uncalibrated) annotation -- the renderer story is a thin adapter, no design debate left.

stories.title: Discriminator A: verify tick thread starts + health-check assertion
stories.intent: Hypothesis A from Spool's discriminator truth-table: the dedicated _powerDownTickThread daemon (US-252 fix) silently never starts, or starts but dies immediately. If true, US-262 logger's pd_tick_count column will stay at 0 throughout Drain 7. This story proves the thread starts AND keeps running by (1) auditing _powerDownTickThread instantiation in HardwareManager + lifecycle to confirm thread.start() is reached and daemon=True; (2) adding a startup journal log line `PowerDownOrchestrator tick thread started, tid=<id>` so journalctl can confirm; (3) adding a health-check that logs pd_tick_count value to journalctl every 60s and RAISES if non-incrementing. After this ships, pd_tick_count behavior post-Drain-7 is fully diagnosable.

src/pi/obdii/orchestrator/lifecycle.py (UPDATE -- audit _powerDownTickThread instantiation path; confirm thread.start() reached + daemon=True; add log line at thread spawn site for symmetry)

stories.completionNotes: Discriminator A liveness instrumentation shipped. THREE artifacts updated + ONE new test file: src/pi/hardware/hardware_manager.py UPDATE (+tickHealthCheckIntervalS constructor parameter default 60.0; +5 _last*/_tickHealthAlarmCount state fields; +tickHealthAlarmCount read-only property; +_checkTickThreadHealth helper that snapshots orchestrator.tickCount on a configurable cadence and logs ERROR + increments alarm counter when count unchanged across full window while on BATTERY; loop entry now logs `tid=<id>` + intervalS at INFO; per-iteration health-check call wrapped in try/except so health-check failure CANNOT halt the tick loop per invariant; thread spawn-site log enriched with daemon + ident; factory reads pi.power.tickHealthCheckIntervalS config; mod-history entry citing Drain-6 rationale; +time module import; ~135 lines net), src/pi/obdii/orchestrator/lifecycle.py UPDATE (audit log line in _wirePowerDownOrchestratorCallbacks confirming the orchestrator was instantiated by HardwareManager and the dedicated tick thread spawn is delegated to HardwareManager._startComponents -- pairs symmetrically with the hardware_manager `tid=<id>` line so a post-Drain-7 forensic walk can prove which side of the boundary failed if the spawn line is missing; mod-history entry; ~20 lines net), tests/pi/hardware/test_power_down_tick_thread_health.py NEW (15 tests across 4 test classes covering: helper-direct discriminator scenarios -- unchanged-count-on-battery raises alarm + ERROR log / on-AC-stalled silent / healthy-increment INFO ok / first-battery-window silent / window-not-elapsed throttled / accumulation across multiple unhealthy windows; property exposure tests -- defaults-to-zero + reflects-internal-counter; constructor parameter tests -- default-60s + override-stored; tick-loop integration tests -- tid-logged-at-entry + battery-noop-raises-alarm + ac-noop-silent + battery-healthy-silent + health-check-raising-does-not-kill-loop. ALL 15 tests would FAIL against pre-fix code per the runtime-validation gate from feedback_runtime_validation_required.md: AttributeError on missing _checkTickThreadHealth + tickHealthAlarmCount, TypeError on missing tickHealthCheckIntervalS kwarg; ~430 lines). SCOPE EXTENSION: tests/pi/power/test_staged_shutdown_actually_fires.py UPDATE (+5-line state-stub addition x3 test methods that bypass __init__ via __new__ and only set the bare-minimum attributes the loop reads; my tickHealthCheckIntervalS state addition broke them on the entry log; this is a direct-consequence test update of an API change, not a tangential drift fix -- documented inline with `# US-265 liveness state required by the tick loop entry log` comment). Verification: tests/pi/hardware/test_power_down_tick_thread_health.py 15/15 PASS in 21.87s; tests/pi/hardware/ + tests/pi/power/ + tests/pi/orchestrator/ + tests/pi/diagnostics/ + tests/pi/integration/ subset 452 passed / 11 skipped / 0 failures in 149.68s (pre-touch was 437 + the 3 staged-shutdown regressions; +15 new + 0 regressions); full fast suite 3833 passed / 26 failed / 17 skipped / 19 deselected in 1006.30s (16:46) -- baseline (Session 136 close per US-264) was 3818 pass / 26 fail, delta +15 = exactly the new test count + ZERO regressions; the 26 failures are the unchanged TD-042 + TD-044 set (closed by US-268 + US-269 later this sprint). Ruff clean across all 4 touched files. sprint_lint US-265 OK (only carryover warning is US-263 sizing). Important runtime-validation note: the discriminator test test_loopOnBatteryWithNoopTick_raisesAlarm mocks orchestrator.tick at the right level per Sprint 19 lesson (Mock with no side_effect so tickCount stays 0); a no-op tick simulates exactly the hypothesis-A pathology -- thread runs the loop body but counter never advances. Pre-Drain-7 this discriminator surface is now fully wired: when CIO runs Drain Test 7 with US-262 logger active, the journalctl ERROR line `PowerDownOrchestrator tick liveness alarm: tickCount=N unchanged across X.Xs while on BATTERY` will fire 60s after the first health-check window if the bug is hypothesis A; concurrent with the logger CSV's pd_tick_count column staying at 0. If that line does NOT appear in journalctl AND pd_tick_count IS incrementing in the CSV, hypothesis A is RULED OUT and the bug is hypothesis B (US-266) or C (US-267). Phantom-path drift continues: scope.filesToRead listed `src/pi/power/orchestrator.py` (US-262 territory); we DID read it for context (the tickCount property contract is the dependency surface) but did not touch it -- explicitly listed in scope.filesToRead. PM action-items.md owns the template-generator audit for the broader phantom-path pattern. Sprint 22 status now: 4/8 shipped (US-262 + US-263 + US-264 + US-265); remaining 4 unclaimed -- US-266 + US-267 discriminator pair (M/S; the other two hypothesis fixes; runtime-validation gate critical for Drain-7 truth-table) + US-268 TD-042 (M; mechanical theme-stub addition across 4 test files; closes 24 TD-042 failures) + US-269 TD-044 (S; one-liner SemVer-shape regex on test_migration_0005). Next-iteration recommended pick: US-266 (M; independent; the discriminator pair lands cleanly together since US-265 covers thread-liveness and US-266 covers gating-logic which are orthogonal in HardwareManager + PowerDownOrchestrator code paths); alternatively US-268 if a quick test-baseline win is preferred (closes the 24 TD-042 failures and brings the fast suite to a fully green baseline before the discriminator story trio completes).

stories.title: Discriminator B: audit tick() gating + DEBUG-log early-returns
stories.intent: Hypothesis B from Spool's discriminator truth-table: pd_tick_count increments (thread alive) but pd_stage stays NORMAL even when VCELL crosses thresholds. Cause class: an early-return guard inside tick() silently bails before reaching the stage-comparison logic -- e.g., `if power_source != BATTERY` returning UNKNOWN, `if vcell is None`, threshold comparison with NoneType, stale-cached VCELL value. This story reads tick() end-to-end, identifies every guard, adds a DEBUG log line at each early-return capturing the value that caused the bail, and writes a synthetic test that would FAIL against pre-fix code if any of these guards is silently swallowing the BATTERY path.

filesToTouch: src/pi/power/orchestrator.py (UPDATE -- read tick() end-to-end; for every conditional that can early-return without advancing stage, add `logger.debug('tick early-return: <reason> value=<x>')`; specifically annotate: power_source != BATTERY, vcell is None, threshold comparison with None, stale-cached VCELL value detection; preserve existing behavior + invariants)

src/pi/hardware/ups_monitor.py (READ-ONLY-TOUCH -- if tick() consumes getPowerSource() return UNKNOWN as a quiet skip-condition, add a one-line comment + DEBUG log at the consumer site only, NOT in ups_monitor)

tests/pi/power/test_tick_gating_no_silent_bail.py (NEW -- 4 parametrized tests, each mocks one guard's bail-condition (BATTERY/UNKNOWN, vcell=None, threshold=None, stale-cache); asserts DEBUG log message captures the value that caused the bail; asserts NO stage transition occurs; this test would FAIL pre-fix because zero DEBUG logs existed at early-return sites)
doNotTouch: PowerDownOrchestrator state-machine logic (US-216 + US-234 territory; correct per Spool)

Common gating-logic failure modes: stale VCELL cache, getPowerSource() returning UNKNOWN treated as 'not BATTERY', NoneType in threshold comparison
Synthetic test must mock at I2cClient.readWord level (Sprint 19 US-235 + Sprint 20 US-245 pattern); high-level mocking would NOT discriminate the bug

Fast suite + new tests pass; ruff clean; sprint_lint 0 errors

DEBUG logs do not fire on the happy-path (must be early-return-only)
Synthetic test FAILS against pre-fix code (no DEBUG logs existed); PASSES against post-fix code
stopConditions: If audit reveals tick() has NO guards that can silently bail (single linear path) -- STOP, document; this story's hypothesis would be invalidated and discriminator-B would be ruled out a-priori

If adding logs reveals an actual bug (e.g., stale VCELL cache discovered during audit) -- STOP, file follow-up story; do NOT fix in-place this sprint (the discriminator design depends on additive-only changes)

feedback.grounding: Audit found 4 silent-bail early-return paths in tick(): (1) thresholds.enabled=False, (2) state==TRIGGER terminal, (3) source==EXTERNAL during NORMAL state (Guard 3 path B = spec stale-cache hypothesis -- upstream getPowerSource() returns cached EXTERNAL when VCELL-history buffer lacks decisive evidence), (4) source!=BATTERY (UNKNOWN -- spec BATTERY/UNKNOWN hypothesis). Each now emits a single logger.debug() line capturing the bail-causing value. Spec hypothesis modes vcell=None / threshold=None were audited and rejected as non-silent: both currently TypeError on threshold comparison and are caught by hardware_manager _powerDownTickLoop except-Exception handler (loud ERROR log, not silent bail). Per the behavior-unchanged invariant no defensive guards added. 5 tests pass post-fix (4 discriminator + 1 happy-path negative invariant); 4 discriminator tests FAIL pre-fix with bailMessages == [] satisfying the runtime-validation gate. Pi subset (363 passed / 11 skipped / 0 failures) confirms zero regressions in pi/power + pi/hardware + pi/diagnostics + pi/integration. Ruff clean. sprint_lint US-266 OK. Discriminator-B wired: post-Drain-7 a journalctl walk for early-return DEBUG lines will name which guard swallowed the BATTERY->WARNING transition (or zero matches will rule out hypothesis B and point at hypothesis C / US-267 write-path).

stories.title: Discriminator C: fsync stage-row writes + audit error propagation
stories.intent: Hypothesis C from Spool's discriminator truth-table: pd_stage advances in CSV (orchestrator state machine fires correctly) but power_log STAGE_* rows never appear -- meaning the write path itself is the gap. SQLite writes can buffer in WAL or commit-but-not-fsync; on hard crash the rows are lost. Additionally, an exception inside power_db.logShutdownStage may be swallowed silently. This story adds explicit conn.commit() + os.fsync(conn.fileno()) (or PRAGMA synchronous=FULL on the write connection) and wraps the INSERT in try/except that LOGs at ERROR level and re-raises -- never swallows. After this ships, the discriminator is conclusive: pd_stage advances + STAGE_* rows present means the ladder finally works.

filesToTouch: src/pi/power/power_db.py (UPDATE -- in logShutdownStage: explicit conn.commit() immediately followed by os.fsync(conn.fileno()) where supported; if SQLite connection does not expose fileno cleanly, set PRAGMA synchronous=FULL on the connection; wrap INSERT in try/except OperationalError + Exception -> log ERROR + re-raise; preserve idempotency)

tests/pi/power/test_logshutdown_stage_fsync_and_error_propagation.py (NEW -- 3 tests: (a) successful logShutdownStage call -> assert os.fsync mock invoked OR PRAGMA synchronous=FULL set; (b) INSERT raises OperationalError -> assert ERROR log + exception re-raised, NOT swallowed; (c) stair-step VCELL test that drives all 3 stage thresholds and asserts power_log has 3 STAGE_* rows AFTER each writer call returns -- the runtime-validation gate)
filesToRead: src/pi/obdii/database.py (SQLite connection setup; PRAGMA + WAL pattern)
tests/pi/power/test_staged_shutdown_actually_fires.py (US-252 stair-step pattern; reuse fixture if possible)
doNotTouch: power_log schema (US-252 territory; vcell column + 3 event_types correct)

groundingRefs.value: If pd_stage advances but power_log has no STAGE_* rows -> this discriminator was the bug
SQLite buffers in WAL by default; hard crash before fsync loses rows
Synthetic test must stair-step VCELL through all 3 thresholds AND assert STAGE_* rows present AFTER writer returns (proves fsync flushed)

acceptance: Pre-flight audit: rg `logShutdownStage|fsync|synchronous|commit\(\)` src/pi/power/power_db.py src/pi/obdii/database.py. Confirm no existing fsync on stage writes.
src/pi/power/power_db.py: logShutdownStage explicit conn.commit() + os.fsync (or PRAGMA synchronous=FULL); INSERT wrapped in try/except that logs ERROR and re-raises -- no swallow

tests/pi/power/test_logshutdown_stage_fsync_and_error_propagation.py: 3 tests cover happy-path fsync invocation + error propagation + stair-step end-to-end with rows-present assertion; all 3 would FAIL pre-fix
Fast suite + new tests pass; ruff clean; sprint_lint 0 errors
invariants: logShutdownStage is idempotent (same stage written twice in close succession does not corrupt state)
fsync or synchronous=FULL adds <50ms per write; no measurable impact on tick-loop cadence
Errors are NEVER swallowed; ERROR log + re-raise is the contract
stopConditions: If SQLite connection-level fsync proves unsupported on the deployed sqlite3 version -- STOP, fall back to PRAGMA synchronous=FULL with explicit commit
If wrapping INSERT in try/except surfaces an actual exception class that Sprint 21 was silently swallowing -- STOP, file follow-up + capture the swallowed exception type

stories.title: TD-044 close: shape-not-literal assert on test_migration_0005
stories.intent: Per TD-044: tests/server/test_migration_0005_dtc_log.py:158 asserts `versions[-1] == '0005'`. After Marcus's Sprint 21 grooming commit `2800c6d` added v0006 (TD-043 hotfix capture), v0006 is now last and the test fails. Same sprint-contract-integrity class as TD-042: a test fixture hard-coded against a then-current literal rather than a shape. Recommendation per TD-044 (option a): tighten the assertion to a SemVer-shape regex (`r'^\d{4}$'`) so the next migration doesn't break it again.
